provenance.title: Exploiting Supply Chain Interdependencies for Stock Return Prediction: A Full-State Graph Convolutional LSTM

authors.name: Chang Liu
authors.affiliation: Department of Information Engineering and Computer Science, University of Trento

provenance.abstract: We propose the Full-State Graph Convolutional LSTM (FS-GCLSTM), a novel temporal graph neural network that incorporates value-chain relationships to enhance stock return forecasting. FS-GCLSTM applies graph convolutions to all LSTM components—current input features, previous hidden states, and cell states.
key_claims: Applying GCN to ALL LSTM inputs (Xt, h_{t-1}, c_{t-1}) outperforms GCN applied only to node features
FS-GCLSTM achieves highest annualized return (7.41%), Sharpe (0.46), Sortino (0.59) on Eurostoxx 600
FS-GCLSTM achieves highest annualized return (9.79%), Sharpe (0.61), Sortino (0.75) on S&P 500
Superior portfolio performance despite NOT having lowest MSE/MAE — portfolio metrics matter more
Performance gains more pronounced in denser Eurostoxx 600 network; stable across sequence lengths 30-120 days

architecture.description: Temporal graph neural network combining Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) with LSTM. Key innovation: GCN applied to ALL LSTM inputs (h_{t-1}, c_{t-1}, X_t) rather than only X_t.

components.name: GraphConvLayer
components.role: Spatial feature extraction via normalized Laplacian convolution (Kipf & Welling 2016)
components.input: Feature matrix X [N, d], adjacency matrix A [N, N]
components.operation: H = f(D_tilde^{-1/2} A_tilde D_tilde^{-1/2} X W), f=ReLU

components.name: FSGCLSTMCell
components.role: Core recurrent cell — applies 2-layer GCN to h_{t-1}, c_{t-1}, X_t before LSTM gates
components.input: X_t [N, d], h_{t-1} [N, hidden], c_{t-1} [N, hidden]
components.operation: See equations f_t, i_t, c_t, o_t, h_t below

components.name: StackedFSGCLSTM
components.role: 3 stacked FS-GCLSTM cells processing temporal sequence of d-day windows
components.input: Sequence of graph snapshots [d, N, features]
components.output: Concatenated final hidden states [3 * N * hidden]

components.name: MLP
components.role: Maps flattened hidden states to per-stock return predictions
components.note: MLP size not specified — ASSUMED: 2 layers with hidden_dim intermediate

architecture.primary_variant: FS-GCLSTM with d=60 input days, 3 stacked cells

equations.name: GCN Layer
equations.where: A_tilde = A + I_n, D_tilde = degree matrix of A_tilde, W learnable, f=ReLU

equations.name: FS-GCLSTM Cell State
equations.role: Key innovation: GCN applied to c_{t-1} as well as h_{t-1} and X_t

data_objects.name: Node feature matrix
data_objects.description: N = number of graph nodes, d = rolling window of daily returns (default: 60)

data_objects.name: Adjacency matrix
data_objects.description: Binary (or confidence-weighted) value-chain supplier-customer relationships, bidirectional

data_objects.name: Hidden state
data_objects.description: Per-node hidden state after GCN transformation

data_objects.name: Cell state
data_objects.description: Per-node cell state — also passed through GCN (key FS-GCLSTM innovation)

data_objects.name: Output predictions
data_objects.description: Predicted next-day returns for N_pred target stocks (N_pred <= N)

training_pipeline.loss_function_note: ASSUMED: MSE is standard for return prediction regression; paper evaluates MSE but does not state training loss explicitly
training_pipeline.batch_size: ASSUMED: full-batch or mini-batch per rolling window — not stated

datasets.name: Eurostoxx 600
datasets.description: 600 large-cap European companies, 17 countries. After filtering: 1576 graph nodes, 550 output nodes, 2501 edges

datasets.name: S&P 500
datasets.description: 500 US companies. After filtering: 1694 nodes, 656 output nodes, 2446 edges

evaluation_protocol.graph_data: LSEG value-chain dataset, confidence score > 20% threshold, bidirectional edges
evaluation_protocol.portfolio_strategy: Daily rebalanced, equal-weighted, long-only. Buy all stocks with positive predicted return. Transaction costs: 1bp. Restricted to current index constituents.

implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard for regression; paper evaluates MSE but does not explicitly state training objective

implementation_assumptions.assumption: MLP has 2 hidden layers with sizes [hidden_dim*2, N_pred]
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper states 'multi-layer perceptron' without specifying layer sizes
Common default for GCN-LSTM; paper does not state hidden dimension

implementation_assumptions.assumption: GCN weight matrix W is shared across the 2 GCN layers applied to each tensor
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard 2-layer GCN as in Kipf & Welling 2016

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Training loss computed only on N_pred output nodes, not all N graph nodes
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper states predictions for N_pred <= N; logical to backpropagate only on target nodes

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Daily log-returns used as node features
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says 'historical price movements'; log-returns are standard

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Batch size equals one full rolling window snapshot
implementation_assumptions.basis: Graph structure makes mini-batching non-trivial; common in graph learning

ambiguities.description: MLP architecture not specified: number of layers, hidden sizes, activation functions
Hidden dimension of GCN and LSTM layers not stated anywhere in the paper
Training objective not explicitly stated. Paper only mentions evaluation metrics (MSE, MAE, etc.)
Batch size not stated. With a dynamic graph, full-batch is common but expensive.
